name: M.S. in Civil Engineering

dimensions.name: Curriculum coherence & progression
dimensions.justification: Only two courses are referenced and both are culminating vehicles (CE 700B Master's Project and CE 701B Master's Thesis); no coursework, concentration, or plan of study is captured, so progression across the degree cannot be evaluated at all. A broad civil M.S. should surface structures, geotechnical, water-resources, transportation, and construction options that are entirely absent here.
dimensions.suggestion: Publish the full plan of study with a core-plus-concentration course structure (structures, geotechnical, environmental/water, transportation, construction) leading into the project/thesis so a coherent trajectory is visible.

dimensions.name: Prerequisite structure health
dimensions.justification: deepestPrereqChain is 0 and missingCourseCount is 0 only because the sole referenced courses are project and thesis credits with no prerequisites; the graph is empty rather than healthy, offering no evidence of sequencing.
dimensions.suggestion: List the graduate coursework and its prerequisites so a minimal but real prerequisite structure (e.g., analysis courses gating advanced electives) appears.

dimensions.name: Learning outcomes & objectives
dimensions.justification: No program-level learning outcomes or objectives are present, and with only thesis/project credits shown the page gives a prospective student nothing to judge intended competencies against.
dimensions.suggestion: Publish 3-5 program learning outcomes describing the analytical and design capabilities the M.S. develops across civil sub-disciplines.

dimensions.name: Credit distribution & balance
dimensions.justification: statedTotalCredits is null and no plan is parsed, so neither the total (an M.S. is typically ~30 credits) nor any split between coursework and the thesis/project is transparent; creditReconcileDelta is uncomputable.
dimensions.suggestion: State the total credit requirement (e.g., 30) and how it divides among core, electives, and thesis/project options.

dimensions.name: Presentation & clarity
dimensions.justification: The entry shows only Master's Project and Master's Thesis; there is no overview, course list, plan of study, or credit summary, so the page cannot orient a prospective applicant to what the degree contains.
dimensions.suggestion: Rebuild the catalog page with a complete course list, plan of study, thesis/non-thesis options, and a credit summary.

dimensions.name: Industry & currency relevance
dimensions.justification: With only project and thesis credits visible, the currency of the degree's actual coursework cannot be assessed; the civil discipline's modern strands (resilience, smart infrastructure, sustainability) are neither confirmed nor visible.
dimensions.suggestion: Surface the concentration areas and their courses so currency (e.g., resilient infrastructure, geospatial methods, sustainable design) is demonstrable.

descriptionQuality.justification: Both referenced courses (CE 700B, CE 701B) carry clear, appropriate descriptions of the project/thesis expectations, but with only two entries there is no basis to judge description quality across the degree's actual coursework.

prerequisiteClarity.justification: No prerequisites beyond advisor approval are stated, and no coursework is present, so prerequisite clarity cannot be meaningfully assessed; the entries are unambiguous only because they are trivially few.

missingTopics.rationale: The page shows only thesis/project credits; the entire coursework spine (structures, geotech, water, transportation, construction) is missing from the catalog data.
No methods, numerical-methods, or advanced-analysis course is referenced to prepare students for the thesis vehicle that is shown.
Contemporary civil M.S. programs foreground climate resilience and sustainable design; none is visible here.
GIS, remote sensing, and simulation are standard civil competencies absent from the referenced content.

emergingTrends.rationale: Civil engineering is reorienting around climate risk; the degree should build and display a resilience research strand.

emergingTrends.trend: Digital delivery and BIM / digital twins
emergingTrends.rationale: Model-based design and digital twins are becoming standard practice the program should position for.
Machine learning for structural health, hydrology, and traffic is a growing research instrument for civil M.S. students.
Low-carbon concrete and smart materials are emerging priorities a civil research degree should reflect.

topSuggestions: Rebuild the catalog entry with the complete coursework plan, since two thesis/project courses cannot represent an M.S. degree.
Publish the core-plus-concentration structure (structures, geotech, water, transportation, construction) and a required research-methods course.
State total credits and the coursework/thesis split to fix the null credit transparency.
Publish program-level learning outcomes describing competencies across civil sub-disciplines.
Surface modern electives (resilience, geospatial/computational methods, sustainable design) so the degree's currency is demonstrable.
